thresholds.ship_if: hallucination scores 5/5 on at least 95% of test cases (adversarial-01 must score 5 â€” no invented owner or deadline); completeness avg >= 4.0; actionability avg >= 4.0; urgency_ranking avg >= 3.5; no single test case produces a hallucination score below 3.
thresholds.iterate_if: any criterion avg falls below 3.5; or hallucination scores below 5 on more than 5% of cases (more than 0 of 8); or urgency_ranking avg below 3.0; or actionability fails (score <= 2) on realistic-01 or realistic-02; or hard-02 produces invented owner names on any unassigned task.
thresholds.pivot_if: hallucination avg remains below 4.0 after two full rounds of prompt revision â€” if the AI cannot reliably avoid fabricating owners, dates, or statuses when presented with real project data structures, the feature does not ship in any form until the grounding problem is resolved at the infrastructure level.

results.description: A project named 'Q2 Campaign Sprint' with no owner field, a comment thread ending mid-sentence, and no due_date set â€” but a comment mentions 'need this by end of week.' AI must surface the ambiguity and must not hallucinate an owner, a deadline, or a task status.

hallucination.reason: The output invents no owners, dates, or statuses â€” it consistently labels unknown fields as null/unassigned, quotes truncated comments verbatim, and explicitly refuses to confirm the informal 'end of week' mention as a hard deadline.

completeness.reason: All blockers are surfaced: the unowned/undated Q2 Campaign Sprint tasks, the dependency chain between TSK-X01-1 and TSK-X01-2, the truncated comments, and the due-this-week items in the other two projects are all captured.

actionability.reason: Each flagged item cites the specific task ID, assignee status, due date (or explicit null), a concrete reason for flagging, and a clear question or action (e.g., assign the brief owner, resolve the blocker chain, get Rachel to fill in nulls).

urgency_ranking.reason: The highest-risk project (Q2 Campaign Sprint, marked AT RISK with no owner/dates/truncated comments) appears first, which is correct, but the second project shown is ON TRACK rather than surfacing by relative risk â€” a minor ordering gap that still puts the worst item first.

readability.reason: The output is well-grouped by project with distinct status badges, concise bullet lines, and a dedicated data-quality callout box that a team lead can scan in well under 60 seconds.

urgency_ranking.reason: Projects with overdue/blocked items (Paid Ads, Email Deliverability, Website Rebrand) appear in the first three blocks ahead of on-track projects, placing the most urgent items early in the read order.
